title: Finnish Salmon Soup (Lohikeitto)
description: A classic Finnish salmon soup — silky, dill-forward, and deeply comforting. Salmon simmered gently in a rich fish stock with leeks, potatoes, carrot, and cream.

ingredients.name: salmon fillet, cut into bite-size chunks (skin reserved for stock)
large leek, trimmed and sliced (white portion only)
salt and white pepper to taste

instructions.text: Make the fish stock: place salmon skins in a small pot with 3 cups of water, a few dill sprigs, and a slice of leek. Bring to a brief boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer for 10 minutes. No rolling boil — it extracts bitterness and cloudiness. Strain and set aside.
Sauté leeks: melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add leeks and cook 8–10 minutes until soft and translucent. If using mushrooms, sauté them separately and set aside.

Add strained fish stock to the leeks. If using mushrooms, add them now. Add potatoes, carrot, and half the dill. Bring to a gentle simmer — small bubbles breaking the surface occasionally, not a churning boil. Simmer 20 minutes until potatoes are just tender.

Add salmon chunks, cream, and nutmeg. Reduce to the lowest simmer possible — boiling at this stage will tighten the fish and risk splitting the dairy. Simmer gently 8–10 minutes until salmon is just cooked through.
If using spinach, stir it in now and let it wilt 1–2 minutes. Add remaining dill. Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Remove from heat and serve immediately. Ladle into bowls and garnish with extra dill if desired.

notes:
Skin the salmon before cutting into chunks — retain the skins for the fish stock. This is the foundation of the soup's flavor.

Heat discipline is everything in this soup: gentle simmer throughout, lowest possible heat once cream and salmon are in. If the surface is actively churning, it's too hot. Occasional bubbles and light steam is correct.

Optional additions: mushrooms integrate well when added with the potatoes; spinach goes in at the very end — any earlier and it goes dull and stringy.

Scales to 6 servings with 1.5 lbs salmon, everything else unchanged.
